title: claude tile data
description: Data for the bundled `claude` tile: one niri desktop. The tile is DATA-DRIVEN — waybar always references `claude`, and the template renders by what you send: a `sessions` array (1 → single layout, 2 → stacked dual layout with a shared shortcut gutter), or an ordinary window (`is_claude:false` → app icon + name). Feed it as the module's `exec` stdout (JSON) or static `text`. Each property notes REAL (from the claude-status-db daemon) vs MOCK (synthesize it).

allOf.comment: A Claude desktop carries sessions[]; an ordinary window carries app/app_icon instead.

then.comment: Window layout: the leftmost window's icon + label.

else.comment: Claude layout: one or two sessions on this desktop.

shortcut.description: MOCK. The niri desktop's shortcut number, shown first (big, in a shared left gutter when two sessions).

active.description: Whether this is the focused/active niri desktop → the accent 'card'.

is_claude.description: true (or omitted) → a Claude desktop (uses `sessions`); false → an ordinary window (uses `app`/`app_icon`).

sessions.description: The Claude session(s) on this desktop (1 → single layout, 2 → stacked dual layout). Send the desktop's sessions; the tile adapts — you never pick the layout, the data does.

state.description: REAL (sessions.state). Drives this session's indicator: working=Claude mascot deep-orange, prompt=blinking '?' (and the WHOLE tile pulses if ANY session is prompt), idle=fade bar (see idle_level) + idle_ago, shell=mascot electric-cyan, empty=dim hollow ring (desktop has no session or window — the producer's absent-desktop placeholder).

idle_ago.description: REAL-derived. When state=idle: time since active (e.g. '12m'), beside the bar. Keep it short.

title.description: MOCK. The session window title. Wrapped (single-session) or tickered (dual).

unpushed.description: MOCK. Unpushed commit count; shown as ↑N after the folder (hidden when 0 or idle).

title.description: Used only when is_claude=false: the window title (shown wrapped under the app). Claude sessions carry their title inside sessions[].title instead.

app.description: Used only when is_claude=false: the app/window label.

app_icon.description: Used only when is_claude=false: a bundled icon name (e.g. 'code') or an absolute path to an .svg.
